2 | security session scoping on coverage graph runtime | security/traceability/maintainability | 0.40 | 0/3/2 | complete |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3368:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:19:- **F004**: Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide — `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154` — `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3370:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:27:| spec_code | fail | hard | `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99` | REQ-012 requires shared graph helpers to scope by `specFolder + loopType + sessionId`, but the underlying write identity is still global by bare `id`. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3373:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:- Handler fallback without `sessionId`: The `all_sessions_default` path is explicitly documented bootstrap/debug behavior rather than a hidden bypass — `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:70`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:174`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3419:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/tasks.md:54:| `MERGE-KEYED` | Merge contracts must key by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, not append order. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3447:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/research.md:75:- `(P2)` Improvement-mode namespace isolation is safer than the packet strategy implied, but for the wrong reason. `mutation-coverage.cjs` writes a local JSON graph with `loopType: 'improvement'`, while the shared SQLite graph only accepts `research` and `review`; improvement data therefore does not pollute the shared store, but the docs overstate a shared-store integration that is not live. [iteration-016.md; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:21; .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157; .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80]\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3448:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/research.md:76:- `(P1)` The shared graph read side still has a session-isolation weakness for research/review sessions: convergence and several query helpers aggregate by `specFolder + loopType` instead of `specFolder + loopType + sessionId`, which is not an improve-agent defect directly but is a real boundary issue uncovered while auditing the improvement namespace claim. [iteration-016.md; .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52; .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126]\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3459:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/research.md:205:5. **Session-scope shared coverage-graph reads for research/review.** Target skill(s): shared graph infrastructure plus loops that consume it. Affected files: `coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph/convergence.ts`, related DB/query helpers, and docs. Rationale: the shared read side aggregates by `specFolder + loopType` instead of session, which can mix separate runs in one packet even if improvement-mode data stays isolated. [iteration-016.md] Suggested approach: propagate `sessionId` through read helpers and convergence decisions wherever the namespace model already allows it.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3474:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7:- The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3497:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/tasks.md:96:- [x] T006 Create `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` bootstrap, schema versioning, migrations, indexes, and namespace isolation by `spec_folder + loop_type + session_id`. (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts`)\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3517:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:42:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3518:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:72:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3600:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1177:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3602:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1263:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3625:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1551:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3648:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1735:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3670:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1779:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3682:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1801:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3694:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1823:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3743:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-state.jsonl:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"config\\\",\\\"mode\\\":\\\"research\\\",\\\"topic\\\":\\\"Further improvements to sk-deep-research v1.5.0.0, sk-deep-review v1.2.0.0, and sk-improve-agent v1.1.0.0 (and their associated commands, agents, YAML workflows): self-compliance audit, coverage graph integration audit, and prioritized recommendations.\\\",\\\"sessionId\\\":\\\"rsr-2026-04-11T08-02-52Z\\\",\\\"parentSessionId\\\":null,\\\"lineageMode\\\":\\\"new\\\",\\\"generation\\\":1,\\\"continuedFromRun\\\":null,\\\"maxIterations\\\":20,\\\"convergenceThreshold\\\":0.05,\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-04-11T08:02:52Z\\\",\\\"specFolder\\\":\\\"skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2\\\"}\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3744:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-state.jsonl:2:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"iteration\\\",\\\"mode\\\":\\\"research\\\",\\\"run\\\":1,\\\"status\\\":\\\"complete\\\",\\\"focus\\\":\\\"D1 \\\"status\\\"],\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3780:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/implementation-summary.md:55:The JSONL merge contract uses explicit key ordering by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId` rather than append order. Segment provenance, dedupe, and conflict metadata survive repeated merges. Segment JSONL lineage is deterministic and auditable.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3785:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-dashboard.md:69:- [x] D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\"improvement\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3786:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-dashboard.md:74:- [x] D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3805:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:8:- The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3812:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:8:- The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\"improvement\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3813:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:9:- The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3815:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:11:- A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3816:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-016.md:12:- The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3844:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7:- The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3896:  - \\\"semantic coverage graph\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3912:Phase 002 turns semantic convergence from an abstract idea into a concrete implementation plan. The coverage graph is the fourth graph system in the existing Spec Kit Memory stack, following the causal graph, the code structural graph, and the graph-signal layer already present under `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3968:Define an implementation-ready semantic coverage graph that reuses existing graph infrastructure, persists loop-scoped graph state in `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`, and feeds graph-backed convergence into the existing Phase 001 stop-decision logic without replacing `newInfoRatio` or other established runtime-truth signals.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4010:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` | Create | SQLite bootstrap, schema versioning, migrations, idempotent upsert support, and snapshot persistence for `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4038:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts` | Create | Verify schema creation, namespace isolation, idempotent upsert behavior, and snapshot persistence. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:4121:  - Requires specFolder, loopType, and sessionId for namespace isolation.',\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7256:   712\\t      specFolder: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Spec folder for namespace isolation (required)' },\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7258:   714\\t      sessionId: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Session identifier for namespace isolation (required)' },\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7292:   748\\t    required: ['specFolder', 'loopType', 'sessionId'],\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7298:   754\\t  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Structured analysis of deep-loop coverage graph state. Supports query types: uncovered_questions (questions with no coverage), unverified_claims (claims without verification), contradictions (CONTRADICTS edge pairs), provenance_chain (BFS from a node following citation/evidence edges), coverage_gaps (nodes missing incoming coverage edges), and hot_nodes (most connected nodes by edge count + weight).',\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7306:   762\\t      sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional session filter' },\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7316:   772\\t  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Report deep-loop coverage graph health: node/edge counts grouped by kind and relation, current convergence signal values, momentum (signal deltas between snapshots), last iteration, schema version, and DB file size. Suitable for dashboards and synthesis surfaces.',\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:7329:   785\\t  description: '[L9:CoverageGraph] Composite convergence assessment for deep-loop coverage graph. Returns a typed decision (CONTINUE, STOP_ALLOWED, STOP_BLOCKED), signal values, blockers with severity levels, and a typed trace explaining each signal threshold evaluation. For research: evaluates questionCoverage, claimVerificationRate, contradictionDensity, plus blocking guards sourceDiversity and evidenceDepth. For review: evaluates dimensionCoverage, findingStability, p0ResolutionRate, evidenceDensity, hotspotSaturation. reviewEdgeFilter = buildSessionFilter('e.session_id', sessionId);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8189:   370\\t    sessionId: r.session_id as string,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8209:Description: Phase 002 defines the namespace as `spec_folder + loop_type + session_id`, but the read-side MCP surface does not enforce that boundary. `deep_loop_graph_query` accepts `sessionId` as optional and explicitly falls back to `all_sessions_default`; `deep_loop_graph_status` and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` do worse, because their published schemas do not expose `sessionId` at all, so callers can only read aggregated cross-session state. That lets one session observe another session’s graph shape and can also contaminate convergence decisions with unrelated session data.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8210:Evidence: The schema for `query` makes `sessionId` optional, while `status` and `convergence` omit it entirely; the handlers then emit `scopeMode: 'all_sessions_default'` and notes describing cross-session fallback when no session is present.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8211:Recommendation: Make `sessionId` required for all non-admin read tools. If aggregate/bootstrap/debug behavior is still needed, expose it through a separate explicitly named debug/admin surface so normal callers cannot bypass session isolation accidentally or maliciously.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8246:1. Trace the reducer and any other runtime callers to confirm whether they always pass `sessionId`, or whether the cross-session read paths are exercised in normal operation.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8262:Description: Phase 002 defines the namespace as `spec_folder + loop_type + session_id`, but the read-side MCP surface does not enforce that boundary. `deep_loop_graph_query` accepts `sessionId` as optional and explicitly falls back to `all_sessions_default`; `deep_loop_graph_status` and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` do worse, because their published schemas do not expose `sessionId` at all, so callers can only read aggregated cross-session state. That lets one session observe another session’s graph shape and can also contaminate convergence decisions with unrelated session data.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8263:Evidence: The schema for `query` makes `sessionId` optional, while `status` and `convergence` omit it entirely; the handlers then emit `scopeMode: 'all_sessions_default'` and notes describing cross-session fallback when no session is present.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8264:Recommendation: Make `sessionId` required for all non-admin read tools. If aggregate/bootstrap/debug behavior is still needed, expose it through a separate explicitly named debug/admin surface so normal callers cannot bypass session isolation accidentally or maliciously.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:8299:1. Trace the reducer and any other runtime callers to confirm whether they always pass `sessionId`, or whether the cross-session read paths are exercised in normal operation.\\n\", truncated_after_lines: None }, duration: 0ns, timed_out: false }, network_policy_decision: None }))" }

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9146:.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md:27:  - `improvement-journal.jsonl` → extracts `lastSessionStart`, `lastSessionEnd`, `totalEvents`, `eventTypeCounts`, and terminal `stopReason` + `sessionOutcome` from the latest session_end event.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9148:.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md:75:| `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml` | Modified | Wired step_emit_journal_event at session_start + iteration + session_end |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9149:.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:267:Event types: `session_start`, `session_initialized`, `integration_scanned`, `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, `benchmark_completed`, `gate_evaluation`, `legal_stop_evaluated`, `blocked_stop`, `promotion_attempt`, `promotion_result`, `rollback`, `rollback_result`, `trade_off_detected`, `mutation_proposed`, `mutation_outcome`, `session_ended`, `session_end`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9150:.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:332:The helper validates event type plus `session_end` or `session_ended` details, and the CLI entrypoint stores boundary context under `details`. Top-level `iteration` and `candidateId` fields are available only through the JS API, not through the CLI wrapper used by the YAML workflows.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9151:.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:340:| `session_end` | Once after synthesis completes or the session reaches a terminal stop | `session_end` | `stopReason`, `sessionOutcome`, `endedAt`, `totalIterations` |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9152:.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:353:- Auto mode emits `session_start` after `step_record_baseline`, then emits `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, and `gate_evaluation` inside each loop iteration, and finally emits `session_end` after synthesis.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9153:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2600:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/checklist.md:34:- [x] CHK-001 [P0] REQ-001 stop contract is documented consistently across both loop products with `stopReason`, `legalStop`, and a legacy mapping table [EVIDENCE: exact artifact paths and JSON field names for `stopReason`, `legalStop.blockedBy`, `legalStop.gateResults`, `legalStop.replayInputs`, plus convergence-doc mapping table] [TESTS: `deep-research-contract-parity.vitest.ts`; `deep-review-contract-parity.vitest.ts`]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9154:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2612:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/implementation-summary.md:39:Both deep-loop products share a typed stop-reason taxonomy with named reasons (`converged`, `max_iterations`, `blocked`, `user_stop`, `error`). The legal-stop gate bundles convergence, coverage, and quality checks into a single binary decision. Blocked-stop events persist with gate results and recovery paths rather than silently terminating. The stop contract uses `stopReason`, `legalStop.blockedBy`, `legalStop.gateResults`, and `legalStop.replayInputs` fields.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9155:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3218:70:- `gateResults` is the replayable legal-stop bundle. Canonical research gates are `convergenceGate`, `coverageGate`, and `qualityGate`; the `qualityGate.checks` map carries the subordinate `sourceDiversity`, `focusAlignment`, and `singleWeakSourceDominance` results.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9156:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3252:214:After composite convergence or full-question coverage nominates STOP, the loop MUST evaluate the full legal-stop bundle before STOP becomes final. STOP is never legal on novelty math alone. The replayable `legalStop.gateResults` bundle for deep research is:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9157:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3253:218:| Convergence Gate | The novelty score stays below `convergenceThreshold` for N consecutive evidence iterations | Block STOP, persist `blocked_stop`, continue |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9158:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3254:219:| Coverage Gate | Every key question has at least one evidence-backed answer | Block STOP, persist `blocked_stop`, continue |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9159:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3255:220:| Quality Gate | Source diversity, focus alignment, and no single weak-source dominance all pass | Block STOP, persist `blocked_stop`, continue |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9160:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3268:274:    gateResults: { convergenceGate, coverageGate, qualityGate },
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9161:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3281:308:2. Append a first-class `blocked_stop` event with `stopReason: "blockedStop"`, `legalStop.blockedBy`, the full `legalStop.gateResults`, and a concrete `recoveryStrategy`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9162:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3291:426:The stop-decision event (`stop_decision` and `blocked_stop` JSONL records) includes which semantic signals supported or prevented STOP:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9163:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3303:468:4.6. **Blocked-stop persistence** (if any legal-stop gate fails, persist `blocked_stop` with recovery strategy and continue)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9164:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3606:85:- `gateResults`: Map of review-specific gate name to pass/fail plus detail. Deep review records `findingStability`, `dimensionCoverage`, `p0Resolution`, `evidenceDensity`, and `hotspotSaturation`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9165:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3644:277:The gate passes only when both the existing churn-based checks AND the semantic stability checks agree. When a semantic check fails, the `legalStop.gateResults.findingStability` detail string includes the semantic signal values.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9166:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3683:403:  blockedBy = [name for name, result in gateResults.items() if not result.pass]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9167:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:3686:411:When convergence math returns STOP, invoke `buildReviewLegalStop()`. If it returns `pass: false`, persist a first-class blocked-stop decision with `stopReason=blockedStop`, populate `legalStop.blockedBy` from the failing gates, copy the full `gateResults`, snapshot the replay inputs, and attach a `recoveryStrategy` describing the next review action before overriding the decision to CONTINUE.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9168:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6734:39:Both deep-loop products share a typed stop-reason taxonomy with named reasons (`converged`, `max_iterations`, `blocked`, `user_stop`, `error`). The legal-stop gate bundles convergence, coverage, and quality checks into a single binary decision. Blocked-stop events persist with gate results and recovery paths rather than silently terminating. The stop contract uses `stopReason`, `legalStop.blockedBy`, `legalStop.gateResults`, and `legalStop.replayInputs` fields.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9169:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:8362:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:411:When convergence math returns STOP, invoke `buildReviewLegalStop()`. If it returns `pass: false`, persist a first-class blocked-stop decision with `stopReason=blockedStop`, populate `legalStop.blockedBy` from the failing gates, copy the full `gateResults`, snapshot the replay inputs, and attach a `recoveryStrategy` describing the next review action before overriding the decision to CONTINUE.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9170:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:8659:4. Audit journal. Today the improver mixes baseline, candidate, rejected, and benchmark records into one state log; that is too coarse for runtime truth. Add `improvement-journal.jsonl` for lifecycle and stop decisions, plus `mutation-ledger.jsonl` for proposal/evaluation outcomes in [scripts/](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts); journal events should include `session_initialized`, `integration_scanned`, `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, `benchmark_completed`, `legal_stop_evaluated`, `blocked_stop`, `promotion_attempted`, `promotion_result`, `rollback_result`, and `session_ended`.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9438:Title: `sk-improve-agent` never emits the first-class legal-stop events that the other reducers depend on
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9439:Description: The improve-agent runtime truth contract says legal-stop evaluation must produce `legal_stop_evaluated` and, on failure, `blocked_stop` with failed gates. The visible auto workflow only emits `gate_evaluation` and `session_end`; the confirm workflow mirrors the same omission at `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_confirm.yaml:198-217`. That leaves `sk-improve-agent` as the outlier versus research/review, where reducer-visible stop-gate events are first-class state. Because those events never exist, the improver reducer cannot replay gate failures, blocked-stop reasons, or blocked-stop history the way the other two reducers can.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9440:Evidence: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md:84-85,104` requires `legal_stop_evaluated` plus `blocked_stop` with full gate results, but the shipped workflow only emits `gate_evaluation` at `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_auto.yaml:168-170` and then jumps to `session_end` at `182-183`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9441:Recommendation: Add explicit `legal_stop_evaluated` and `blocked_stop` journal emissions to both improve-agent workflows, include the gate bundle and failed-gate list in the emitted payload, and extend the reducer with a `blockedStopHistory`-style consumer/dashboard section so the improver loop matches research/review runtime-truth replay.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9443:2. `F-017-002` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `835-844, 761-813`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9444:Title: Improve-agent reducer reads replay artifacts into the registry but drops them from the dashboard
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9445:Description: The reducer loads `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage` into the registry, but `renderDashboard()` never renders any of them. That breaks the shipped Phase 008 contract that operators should see the replay-consumer summaries in the dashboard itself, not only in raw JSON. Research/review dashboards both surface their reducer-derived runtime-truth sections; improve-agent currently hides its equivalent data.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9446:Evidence: The reducer stores the fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:837-839`, but `renderDashboard()` only outputs Global Summary, Sample Quality, Guardrails, Stop Status, per-profile sections, and Recommendation at `761-813`. In the low-sample fixture, the reducer produces non-null `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage`, while the rendered dashboard only contains `## Sample Quality`. This also contradicts `.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md:85`, which says the dashboard exposes the “full journal/lineage/coverage summary.”
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9447:Recommendation: Add dedicated dashboard sections for journal summary, candidate lineage, and mutation coverage, and add a regression test that asserts those sections appear when the replay artifacts are present and degrade cleanly to `null`/`n/a` when absent.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9449:3. `F-017-003` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `217-237`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9451:Description: The research reducer only accepts `signals.blendedScore` or `signals.score`. The state-format contract says `blendedScore` is included “when available,” which makes it optional. When a valid `graph_convergence` event carries only the typed signal bundle (`questionCoverage`, `claimVerificationRate`, `contradictionDensity`, etc.), the reducer still records the decision and blockers but forces `graphConvergenceScore` to `0`. That produces a false “no graph support” signal in the registry/dashboard and diverges from the review reducer, which already handles alternate names and numeric fallback.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9452:Evidence: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:317` says `signals` includes `blendedScore` “when available.” With the same event payload `{questionCoverage:0.86, claimVerificationRate:0.78, contradictionDensity:0.04}`, `sk-deep-research` returns `{graphConvergenceScore:0}`, while `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:392-414` computes `0.56` from the numeric signals.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9455:4. `F-017-004` | Severity: `P2` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `437-446, 857-861`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9456:Title: Review dashboard’s `ACTIVE RISKS` path bypasses blocker normalization and can regress to `[object Object]`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9457:Description: The reducer added `normalizeBlockedByList()` specifically so legacy structured blocker entries do not render as `[object Object]` in the dashboard. The `BLOCKED STOPS` section uses the normalized history, but `ACTIVE RISKS` bypasses that work and joins `latestBlockedStop.blockedBy` directly from the raw JSONL event. If a legacy packet or graph-blocker payload still carries structured entries, the main risk summary can regress even though the blocked-stop section is fixed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9458:Evidence: The normalization helper is explicitly documented at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:437-445`, but `ACTIVE RISKS` reads the raw event and does `latestBlockedStop.blockedBy.join(', ')` at `857-861` instead of using `buildBlockedStopHistory()` output.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9459:Recommendation: Reuse the normalized entry from `registry.blockedStopHistory.at(-1)` inside `ACTIVE RISKS`, or call `normalizeBlockedByList()` there before rendering.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9466:Reviewed about `95%` of the requested focus area: all three `reduce-state.cjs` implementations, the improve-agent journal helper and visible YAML emitters, the 042 packet phase docs/changelog claims tied to reducer/runtime-truth behavior, and reducer smoke checks on live packet/fixture data. I did not do a full mirror sweep of `.claude/`, `.codex/`, and `.gemini` runtime mirrors in this iteration.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9472:1. Verify the improve-agent end-to-end write path for `legal_stop_evaluated` / `blocked_stop` with a real fixture or workflow run, then add reducer/dashboard assertions for those events.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9483:Title: `sk-improve-agent` never emits the first-class legal-stop events that the other reducers depend on
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9484:Description: The improve-agent runtime truth contract says legal-stop evaluation must produce `legal_stop_evaluated` and, on failure, `blocked_stop` with failed gates. The visible auto workflow only emits `gate_evaluation` and `session_end`; the confirm workflow mirrors the same omission at `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_confirm.yaml:198-217`. That leaves `sk-improve-agent` as the outlier versus research/review, where reducer-visible stop-gate events are first-class state. Because those events never exist, the improver reducer cannot replay gate failures, blocked-stop reasons, or blocked-stop history the way the other two reducers can.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9485:Evidence: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md:84-85,104` requires `legal_stop_evaluated` plus `blocked_stop` with full gate results, but the shipped workflow only emits `gate_evaluation` at `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_auto.yaml:168-170` and then jumps to `session_end` at `182-183`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9486:Recommendation: Add explicit `legal_stop_evaluated` and `blocked_stop` journal emissions to both improve-agent workflows, include the gate bundle and failed-gate list in the emitted payload, and extend the reducer with a `blockedStopHistory`-style consumer/dashboard section so the improver loop matches research/review runtime-truth replay.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9488:2. `F-017-002` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `835-844, 761-813`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9489:Title: Improve-agent reducer reads replay artifacts into the registry but drops them from the dashboard
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9490:Description: The reducer loads `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage` into the registry, but `renderDashboard()` never renders any of them. That breaks the shipped Phase 008 contract that operators should see the replay-consumer summaries in the dashboard itself, not only in raw JSON. Research/review dashboards both surface their reducer-derived runtime-truth sections; improve-agent currently hides its equivalent data.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9491:Evidence: The reducer stores the fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:837-839`, but `renderDashboard()` only outputs Global Summary, Sample Quality, Guardrails, Stop Status, per-profile sections, and Recommendation at `761-813`. In the low-sample fixture, the reducer produces non-null `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage`, while the rendered dashboard only contains `## Sample Quality`. This also contradicts `.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md:85`, which says the dashboard exposes the “full journal/lineage/coverage summary.”
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9492:Recommendation: Add dedicated dashboard sections for journal summary, candidate lineage, and mutation coverage, and add a regression test that asserts those sections appear when the replay artifacts are present and degrade cleanly to `null`/`n/a` when absent.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9494:3. `F-017-003` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `217-237`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-017.md:9496:Description: The research reducer only accepts `signals.blendedScore` or `signals.score`. The state-format contract says `blendedScore` is included “when available,” which makes it optional. When a valid `graph_convergence` event carries only the typed signal bundle (`questionCoverage`, `claimVerificationRate`, `contradictionDensity`, etc.), the reducer still records the decision and blockers but forces `graphConvergenceScore` to `0`. That produces a false “no graph support” signal in the registry/dashboard and diverges from the review reducer, which already handles alternate names and numeric fallback.

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_resume_auto.yaml:4:role: Expert Developer using Smart SpecKit for autonomous session resume

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_resume_auto.yaml:19:resume_philosophy:

.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:363:- `handover.md` → `@handover` agent exclusively (session continuation documents)
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:531:- Use `@handover` to create a continuation document first
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:600:| Session ending or user says "stopping" | `/spec_kit:handover` | Preserve context for continuation      |
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:604:| Resuming prior work (known spec)       | `/spec_kit:resume`   | Recover via `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> spec docs |
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:605:| Resuming interrupted work (unknown)    | `/spec_kit:resume`   | Auto-detect the packet, then follow the same canonical recovery order |
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:784:| `/spec_kit:handover`        | Session continuation                            | `.opencode/command/spec_kit/handover.md`     |
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:788:| `/spec_kit:resume`         | Resume work or recover interrupted session      | `.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md`       |
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:810:1. Use `/spec_kit:resume` semantics: recover from `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then the packet's spec docs
.opencode/agent/orchestrate.md:811:2. Use `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` only when packet location or continuity state is still unclear
.codex/agents/deep-research.toml:69:- Lifecycle branch from `config.lineage.lineageMode` (`new`, `resume`, or `restart`). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred — see `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches`.
.codex/agents/deep-research.toml:434:If hook-injected context is present (from the runtime startup/bootstrap surface), use it directly. Do NOT redundantly call `memory_context` or `memory_match_triggers` for the same information. If hook context is NOT present, fall back to: `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` then `memory_match_triggers()`.
.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:49:    │   │ To proceed, restart with:                                  │
.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:339:Supported lineage modes: `new`. sk-improve-agent is one-session-only in the current release — every invocation starts a fresh session with generation 1. `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no runtime wiring; see `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md §Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)` for the full retraction.
.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:444:- Skipped integration scan before candidate generation
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_dashboard.md:18:3. **Resume decision** — in confirm mode, shown to user before resume/restart/fork choice
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_dashboard.md:36:- Session: [from config.sessionId] (generation [from config.generation], lineage [from config.lineageMode])
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/deep_research_strategy.md:22:- **Protection:** Shared state with explicit ownership boundaries. Orchestrator validates consistency on resume.
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/deep_research_strategy.md:127:- Lifecycle branches: `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/deep_research_strategy.md:133:- Current generation: [from config.lineage.generation]
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:336:- `resume`: Continue the active review session; same `sessionId`, no archive. The workflow appends a typed `resumed` JSONL event before dispatch.
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:337:- `restart`: Archive the existing `review/` tree under `review_archive/{timestamp}/`, mint a fresh `sessionId`, increment `generation`. The workflow appends a typed `restarted` JSONL event with a non-null `archivedPath`.
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:340:- `fork`: Earlier drafts described this as a child review session from an earlier lineage point. Not emitted today.
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:341:- `completed-continue`: Earlier drafts described re-opening a completed session for additional review coverage. Not emitted today.
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:348:- `lineageMode`
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:349:- `generation`
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:350:- `continuedFromRun`
.codex/agents/deep-review.toml:573:If hook-injected context is present (from the runtime startup/bootstrap surface), use it directly. Do NOT redundantly call `memory_context` or `memory_match_triggers` for the same information. If hook context is NOT present, fall back to: `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` then `memory_match_triggers()`.

.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md:52:- `restart` — archive the existing `research/` tree under `research_archive/{timestamp}/`, mint a fresh `sessionId`, increment `generation`, and append a typed `restarted` JSONL event with the same field set plus a non-null `archivedPath`

.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:78:- Lifecycle branch from `config.lineage.lineageMode` (`new`, `resume`, or `restart`). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred — see `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches`.
.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:442:If hook-injected context is present (from Claude Code SessionStart hook), use it directly. Do NOT redundantly call `memory_context` or `memory_match_triggers` for the same information. If hook context is NOT present, rebuild the active packet context from `handover.md`, then the active spec doc's `_memory.continuity`, then the relevant spec docs. Only widen to `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` and `memory_match_triggers()` when those canonical packet sources are missing or insufficient.
.opencode/agent/deep-research.md:444:Route queries by intent: CocoIndex (`mcp__cocoindex_code__search`) for semantic discovery, Code Graph (`code_graph_query`/`code_graph_context`) for structural navigation, canonical packet continuity (`handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> spec docs, or the operator-facing `/spec_kit:resume` output) for active-session recovery, and Memory (`memory_search`/`memory_context`) for broader historical context after the packet sources are exhausted.
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:358:- `handover.md` → `@handover` agent exclusively (session continuation documents)
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:526:- Use `@handover` to create a continuation document first
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:595:| Session ending or user says "stopping" | `/spec_kit:handover` | Preserve context for continuation      |
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:599:| Resuming prior work (known spec)       | `/spec_kit:resume`   | Load context from spec folder          |
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:600:| Resuming interrupted work (unknown)    | `/spec_kit:resume`   | Auto-detect recent work or recover interrupted sessions |
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:779:| `/spec_kit:handover`        | Session continuation                            | `.opencode/command/spec_kit/handover.md`     |
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:783:| `/spec_kit:resume`          | Resume or recover work, including crash restoration | `.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md`   |
.claude/agents/orchestrate.md:805:1. `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` for session recovery

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:33:  lineage_mode: "[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:160:          resume: "Config, JSONL, and strategy all exist and config.mode == 'review' and agree on target/spec folder"

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:168:          options: [resume, restart, cancel]
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:169:          on_resume_choice:

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:175:          on_restart_choice:

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:180:              generation: "{prior_generation + 1}"
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:181:            append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{spec_folder}/review_archive/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:186:          note: "fork and completed-continue branches are deferred. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are runtime-supported in this release. See sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md `Session Lifecycle` for the canonical one-session model."

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:420:               blocked_gates = ["claimAdjudicationGate"]

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:423:          1. HARD STOPS (each still subject to step 5 legal-stop gates, including claimAdjudicationGate):
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:432:               If graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED", set stop_blocked = true and route through blocked_stop emission instead of stopping.
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:453:               f) claimAdjudicationGate: pass when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == true (i.e. last_claim_adjudication_passed != false OR there are no active P0/P1 findings to adjudicate); record last_claim_adjudication_passed and claim_adjudication_active_count
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:458:               blocked_by_json = JSON array of failing gate names (always include "claimAdjudicationGate" when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == false)
.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:472:               recovery_strategy = one-line hint describing the next review action needed to satisfy the failed gates (when claimAdjudicationGate is the failing gate, prefer the step-0 recovery_strategy already set)

.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:1020:      - "/spec_kit:handover {spec_folder} (prepare the canonical resume packet for the next session)"
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:267:Event types: `session_start`, `session_initialized`, `integration_scanned`, `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, `benchmark_completed`, `gate_evaluation`, `legal_stop_evaluated`, `blocked_stop`, `promotion_attempt`, `promotion_result`, `rollback`, `rollback_result`, `trade_off_detected`, `mutation_proposed`, `mutation_outcome`, `session_ended`, `session_end`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:285:Sessions support a single lineage mode today: `new`. Every invocation of the `/improve:agent` workflow starts a fresh session with a new session id and generation 1. Multi-generation lineage modes (`resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`) were described in earlier drafts but have no shipped runtime wiring in the improve-agent workflow, reducer, or journal consumer.
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:339:| `iteration_boundary` | On every iteration after candidate generation, after candidate scoring, and after gate evaluation | `candidate_generated`, `candidate_scored`, `gate_evaluation` | Per-iteration context such as `sessionId`, `iteration`, `candidateId`, `candidatePath`, `scoreOutputPath`, `weightedScore`, and gate decision details |
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:377:- `journalSummary`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:378:- `candidateLineage`
.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:379:- `mutationCoverage`
.codex/agents/handover.toml:12:Session handover specialist responsible for creating continuation documents that enable seamless session branching.
.codex/agents/handover.toml:20:**IMPORTANT**: This agent is dispatched by `/spec_kit:handover`. It owns context gathering and handover generation while the main agent owns user interaction and validation.
.codex/agents/handover.toml:182:| `/spec_kit:resume`   | Loads handover.md created by this agent   |
.codex/agents/handover.toml:202:If hook-injected context is present (from the runtime startup/bootstrap surface), use it directly. Do NOT redundantly call `memory_context` or `memory_match_triggers` for the same information. If hook context is NOT present, fall back to: `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` then `memory_match_triggers()`.
.codex/agents/handover.toml:217:│  ├─► Handover document generation with template                         │
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:269:- `resume` — continue the active lineage; appends a typed `resumed` JSONL event with `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `continuedFromRun`, `generation`, `archivedPath` (null), `timestamp`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:270:- `restart` — archive the existing `review/` tree under `review_archive/{timestamp}/`, mint a fresh `sessionId`, increment `generation`, and append a typed `restarted` JSONL event with the same field set plus a non-null `archivedPath`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:273:- `fork` — earlier drafts described a sibling-lineage branch; the workflow no longer exposes this option
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:274:- `completed-continue` — earlier drafts described snapshotting `review-report-v{generation}.md`; not runtime-wired
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:281:- `lineageMode`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:282:- `generation`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:283:- `continuedFromRun`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:451:- **Continuity**: `/spec_kit:resume` is the operator-facing recovery surface; canonical packet continuity is written via `generate-context.js`
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:458:  /spec_kit:resume
.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md:478:| `/spec_kit:resume` | Canonical recovery surface before resuming or extending an active review packet |
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:11:  - "spec kit resume"
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:61:| **handover** | `/spec_kit:handover [spec-folder]` | varies | Create session handover document for continuation |
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:62:| **resume** | `/spec_kit:resume [spec-folder] [:auto\|:confirm]` | varies | Resume or recover work on an existing spec folder |
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:75:| `resume` | Existing spec folder with saved state or recoverable session context |
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:95:├── resume.md         # /spec_kit:resume - Resume existing work
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:110:    ├── spec_kit_resume_auto.yaml
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:111:    └── spec_kit_resume_confirm.yaml
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:142:resume (continue in new session)
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:157:| resume | Loads memory context, continues from last state |
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:208:# Create handover for session continuation
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:212:/spec_kit:resume specs/012-rate-limiting :auto
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:233:**Q: Can I resume a spec folder that was never explicitly handed over?**
.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt:235:Yes. `/spec_kit:resume` loads the best available continuation context for the spec folder even if you never wrote a handover. The canonical recovery ladder is `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> canonical spec docs. If one rung is missing, resume continues with the next packet-local source. If no saved state exists anywhere in that ladder, the command prompts you to start fresh with `/spec_kit:plan`. Running `/spec_kit:handover` before ending a session still improves the first recovery pass, but it is not required.

.opencode/agent/write.md:89:**If the documentation task continues prior packet work and no Context Package is provided**: Rebuild the active context from `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then the relevant spec docs or the current `/spec_kit:resume` output. Use generated `memory/*.md` only as supporting artifacts when they already exist; they are not the canonical continuity surface.

.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:27:> Examples: `/spec_kit:resume specs/007-feature/` | `/spec_kit:resume:auto specs/007-feature/`
.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:70:     Present phase selection to user so they can choose which phase to resume
.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:74:   - Detected → continuation_detected = TRUE, parse Last/Next
.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:75:   - Not detected → continuation_detected = FALSE

.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:102:     A) Yes, resume  B) Select different folder  C) Cancel

.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:113:     A) Fast resume - just enough context to continue safely
.opencode/command/spec_kit/resume.md:126:   - continuation_choice = [from Q2, or N/A]
