   152	<!-- ANCHOR:risks -->
   153	## 6. RISKS & DEPENDENCIES
   154	
   155	| Type | Item | Impact | Mitigation |
   156	|------|------|--------|------------|
   157	| Dependency | Phase 1 is the contract foundation for every later phase | High | Treat Phase 1 completion as the first milestone and handoff gate. |
   158	| Dependency | Phase 2 graph tooling must exist before graph-enhanced segmentation is trustworthy | High | Keep Phase 3 graph-aware work blocked until Phase 2 is operational. |
   159	| Dependency | Fan-out/join capability is not yet proven on the current YAML engine | High | Keep Phase 3 wave execution gated behind an explicit prerequisite proof. |
   160	| Dependency | Replay fixtures and behavioral suites are prerequisites for non-advisory optimization | High | Keep Phase 4a advisory-only and Phase 4b deferred until those prerequisites exist. |
   161	| Risk | Parent docs drift back into duplicated child detail | Medium | Keep the root packet as an index/overview and push implementation detail into child folders only. |
   162	| Risk | Optional modes are mistaken for baseline requirements | Medium | Keep council mode, coordination-board work, and Phase 4b explicitly marked opt-in or deferred. |
   163	| Risk | Cross-surface parity drifts across commands, skills, agents, reducers, and mirrors | Medium | Use child-phase parity/test gates and keep the parent focused on sequencing and dependency visibility. |
   164	<!-- /ANCHOR:risks -->
   165	
   166	---
   167	
   168	## 7. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
   169	
   170	### Performance
   171	
   172	- Parent docs should stay concise enough to scan faster than the child packets.
   173	- Parent milestone and requirement mapping should not require child-packet duplication to stay understandable.
   174	
   175	### Reliability
   176	
   177	- Parent links must continue to point to the correct child sources of truth.
   178	- Deferred work must remain clearly marked so later readers do not treat it as active delivery.
   179	
   180	### Maintainability
   181	
   182	- The root packet should summarize and route, not compete with child docs.
   183	- Cross-phase sequencing should be changeable in one place at the root without rewriting child packets.
   184	
   185	---
   186	
   187	## 8. EDGE CASES
   188	
   189	- Phase 3 planning advances before fan-out/join is proven: keep wave execution blocked until the proof path exists.
   190	- Phase 4b is mistaken for active scope: keep it labeled deferred in every parent overview surface.
   191	- A child phase changes counts or status: update the parent index instead of re-copying child prose.
   192

    63	## 3. ARCHITECTURE
    64	
    65	### Pattern
    66	
    67	Phase-gated delivery with child-packet ownership of detail and parent-packet ownership of sequencing, milestones, and handoffs.
    68	
    69	### Phase Dependency Graph
    70	
    71	```text
    72	Phase 1: Runtime Truth Foundation
    73	  -> Phase 2: Semantic Coverage Graph
    74	  -> Phase 3: Wave Executor
    75	  -> Phase 4: Offline Loop Optimizer
    76	```
    77

    79

    81	|-----------|----------------|--------------|
    82	| **M1** | Phase 1 complete and validated | Runtime truth foundation |
    83	| **M2** | Phase 2 complete and validated | Semantic coverage graph |
    84	| **M3** | Phase 3 complete and validated | Wave executor |
    85	| **M4** | Phase 4a complete and validated | Offline deterministic optimizer |
    86	
    87	### Consolidated Effort
    88	
    89	| Area | Effort Posture |
    90	|------|----------------|
    91	| **Phase 1** | Largest foundation wave; carries the heaviest contract and verification load |
    92	| **Phase 2** | Large integration wave; graph reuse plus new MCP/reducer seams |
    93	| **Phase 3** | Medium-high orchestration wave with a hard prerequisite proof |
    94	| **Phase 4a** | Narrower implementation wave; offline and advisory-only by design |
    95	| **Phase 4b** | Deferred; estimate intentionally withheld until prerequisites exist |
    96	
    97	**Active total estimate**: approximately 33-39 focused implementation sessions across Phases 1, 2, 3, and 4a.

    13	# Implementation Summary: Deep Research and Deep Review Runtime Improvement Bundle
    14	
    15	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->

    17	
    18	---
    19	
    20	<!-- ANCHOR:metadata -->
    21	## Metadata
    22	
    23	| Field | Value |
    24	|-------|-------|
    25	| **Spec Folder** | 042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2 |
    26	| **Completed** | 2026-04-10 |
    27	| **Level** | 3 |
    28	| **Execution Model** | Four child phases; Phase 4 split into active `4a` (complete) and deferred `4b` |
    29	<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->
    30	
    31	---
    32	
    33	<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->
    34	## What Was Built
    35	
    36	The deep-loop stack is now auditable, graph-aware, scale-ready, and offline-tunable. This bundle converted consolidated agentic-systems research into four implementation phases that upgraded `sk-deep-research` and `sk-deep-review` without collapsing them into a generic workflow. The work touched approximately 100 files and added over 19,000 lines across contracts, libraries, MCP tools, reducer logic, agent instructions, and test suites.
    37	
    38	### Phase 1: Runtime Truth Foundation
    39	
    40	Every loop stop now reports a named reason from a shared taxonomy, passes through a legal-stop gate checking convergence + coverage + quality together, and carries resume semantics for deterministic continuation. Separate append-only journals, semantic convergence traces, and reducer ownership boundaries make loop behavior explicitly auditable. 44 files changed, +7K lines, verified through 3 rounds of deep review.
    41	
    42	### Phase 2: Semantic Coverage Graph
    43	
    44	Convergence moved from abstract concept to concrete graph substrate. Four CJS shared libraries extract and adapt graph primitives from existing memory MCP code. A dedicated `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` stores coverage nodes, edges, and snapshots. Four MCP tools (`upsert`, `query`, `status`, `convergence`) expose graph state to reducers. The reducer/MCP contract defines explicit authority order and fallback chain. 25 files (17 new), +5.2K lines, 101 tests.
    45	
    46	### Phase 3: Wave Executor
    47	
    48	Large targets can now be segmented and processed in parallel waves without turning LEAF agents into sub-agent managers. Helper-module orchestration provides fan-out/join capability. Deterministic heuristic segmentation (v1) and graph-enhanced segmentation (v2) produce reproducible segment plans. Activation gates keep wave mode bounded to genuinely large targets (1000+ files / 50+ domains). Keyed merge preserves provenance and dedupe. 11 files (9 new), +3.3K lines, 97 tests.
    49	
    50	### Phase 4: Offline Loop Optimizer
    51	
    52	Phase 4a delivers a complete offline compile/evaluate loop that tunes deterministic numeric configs against real `040` packet traces, scores them with a multi-dimensional rubric, and emits advisory-only candidate patches with full audit trails. The promotion gate refuses production mutation until replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist. Phase 4b (prompt-pack generation, meta-learning, automatic promotion) remains explicitly deferred. 20 files (14 new), +3.8K lines, 91 tests.
    53	<!-- /ANCHOR:what-built -->
    54	
    55	---
    56	
    57	<!-- ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
    58	## How It Was Delivered
    59	
    60	Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.
    61	
    62	After the four foundation phases landed, the packet took on four follow-up phases that kept the deep-loop skills honest with their shipped runtime:
    63	
    64	- **Phase 005 — agent-improver deep-loop alignment.** Brought sk-improve-agent into parity with the frozen stop-reason taxonomy, journal wiring, and advisory optimizer contract.
    65	- **Phase 006 — graph testing and playbook alignment.** Added vitest coverage across the coverage-graph layers and authored matching playbook scenarios so manual QA mirrors the automated checks.
    66	- **Phase 007 — graph-aware stop gate plus runtime truth reconciliation.** Wired the graph convergence MCP calls into both loop workflows and reconciled the fail-closed reducer behavior with the visible state-log contract.
    67	- **Phase 008 — further deep-loop improvements.** Closed the 12 Codex-research recommendations plus the 4 phase-008 closing-audit P1 findings (commit `c07c9fbcf`) and shipped 12 new vitest tests, 7 playbook scenarios, and SKILL.md + changelog bumps for all three deep-loop skills.
    68	
    69	After phase 008 landed, a **10-iteration Codex GPT-5.4 `spec_kit:deep-review` closing audit** (`rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z`) surfaced 16 residual findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) that Parts A–D did not catch. The verdict was CONDITIONAL, and the full `review/` packet routed into a five-lane remediation pass that landed in the same packet:
    70	
    71	1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.
    72	2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.
    73	3. **Lane 3 — Lifecycle Persistence** closed F010, F011, and F012 by retracting the unimplemented `fork` and `completed-continue` branches from the review + research confirm workflows, wiring real event emission for `resumed` and `restarted`, and narrowing sk-improve-agent's `Resume/Continuation Semantics` to the shipped one-session model. New REQs: REQ-030, REQ-031.
    74	4. **Lane 4 — Canonical Contract Cleanup** closed F001, F003, F008, and F009 by rewriting the canonical `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md` iteration skeleton to match `reduce-state.cjs:186`, adding `review_dimensions_json` pre-serialization in both review workflows, replacing the phantom `legalStop` record in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md` with the canonical persisted `blocked_stop` event shape, and correcting Signal 3 of the 3-signal convergence vote to `Dimension Coverage`. New REQs: REQ-032, REQ-033.
    75	5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.
    76	
    77	Each lane shipped as an independent commit with targeted vitest and tsc verification. The closing audit is therefore the source of truth for what was still outstanding after phase 008, and the five-lane remediation is the source of truth for how the outstanding debt was absorbed.
    78	<!-- /ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
    79	
    80	---
    81	
    82	<!-- ANCHOR:decisions -->
    83	## Key Decisions
    84	
    85	| Decision | Why |
    86	|----------|-----|
    87	| Phase the work into four child packets | Keep the root packet concise and make each area independently executable and verifiable |
    88	| Split Phase 4 into active `4a` and deferred `4b` | Keep deterministic optimizer work in scope now and postpone unsafe prompt/meta optimization |
    89	| Keep deep research and deep review as separate products | Collapsing them into a generic workflow DSL would lose product-specific semantics |
    90	| Shared stop-reason taxonomy with legal-stop gates | One auditable vocabulary plus explicit enforcement prevents hidden runtime state |
    91	| Reuse-first graph extraction with explicit adaptation boundaries | Avoids greenfield duplication while being honest about what transfers and what does not |
    92	| Orchestrator-layer wave execution, not LEAF-agent spawning | Workers stay LEAF; scale responsibility belongs at the orchestration layer |
    93	| Advisory-only optimizer outputs | No production config changes without human review and prerequisite validation |
    94	<!-- /ANCHOR:decisions -->
    95	
    96	---
    97	
    98	<!-- ANCHOR:verification -->
    99	## Verification
   100	
   101	| Check | Result |
   102	|-------|--------|
   103	| Phase 1: Runtime truth contracts and behavior tests | PASS |
   104	| Phase 2: Coverage graph libraries, MCP tools, and 101 tests | PASS |
   105	| Phase 3: Wave executor, keyed merge, and 97 tests | PASS |
   106	| Phase 4a: Replay optimizer, audit trail, and 91 tests | PASS |
   107	| Phase 4b: Remains blocked with explicit prerequisites | PASS |
   108	| Phase 5: agent-improver deep-loop alignment | PASS |
   109	| Phase 6: graph testing and playbook alignment | PASS |
   110	| Phase 7: graph-aware stop gate + runtime-truth reconciliation | PASS |
   111	| Phase 8: further deep-loop improvements (12 Codex research recs + 4 phase-008 closing-audit P1 fixes) | PASS |
   112	| Cross-phase deep review (3 rounds in phases 1–4, 0 P0 / 3 P1 closed) | PASS |
   113	| Phase-008 closing deep-review (10 Codex iterations, `rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z`, CONDITIONAL) | PASS with 16 findings routed to Lane 1–5 remediation |
   114	| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |
   115	| Post-remediation vitest scripts/tests run | PASS — 908 passed / 55 skipped / 0 failed |
   116	| Post-remediation `tsc --noEmit` on mcp_server | PASS |
   117	| Parent-child doc synchronization | PASS — this summary, `spec.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, and the phase-008 summary were all reconciled as the final action of Lane 5 |
   118	<!-- /ANCHOR:verification -->
   119	
   120	---
   121	
   122	<!-- ANCHOR:limitations -->
   123	## Known Limitations
   124	
   125	1. **Phase 4b is deferred.** Prompt-pack generation, cross-packet meta-learning, and automatic promotion remain blocked until behavioral test suites and 2+ compatible corpus families exist.
   126	2. **Wave mode has not been exercised on production-scale targets.** Activation thresholds are design estimates pending real-world validation.
   127	3. **Graph weight calibration uses initial estimates.** Coverage-specific edge weights will be refined as production run data accumulates.
   128	4. **Advisory-only optimizer outputs require human review.** No candidate config change is applied to production automatically.
   129	<!-- /ANCHOR:limitations -->

     5	
     6	# Deep Research Dashboard - Session Overview
     7	
     8	Auto-generated from JSONL state log and strategy file. Regenerated after every iteration evaluation. Never manually edited.
     9	
    10	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    11	## 1. OVERVIEW
    12	
    13	### Purpose
    14	
    15	Provides a compact, human-readable summary of the current research session. Serves three roles:
    16	1. **Observability** — quick status check during long autonomous runs
    17	2. **Recovery surface** — second recovery channel alongside JSONL after context compaction
    18	3. **Lifecycle decision** — in confirm mode, shown before `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, or `completed-continue`
    19	
    20	### Usage
    21	
    22	- **Location:** `{spec_folder}/research/deep-research-dashboard.md`
    23	- **Generated by:** Orchestrator (step_generate_dashboard in YAML workflow)
    24	- **Data sources:** `deep-research-state.jsonl` + `findings-registry.json` + `deep-research-strategy.md`
    25	- **Protection:** `auto-generated` — overwritten each iteration, never manually edited

 succeeded in 0ms:
   524	   {"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"rvw-2026-03-24T10-00-00Z","parentSessionId":"rvw-2026-03-24T10-00-00Z","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":4,"generation":1,"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:05:00Z"}
   525	   ```
   526	6. **Continue**: Enter the iteration loop from step_read_state
   527	
   528	### Lifecycle Branches (current release)
   529	
   530	The runtime supports three lineage modes today. `fork` and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no workflow wiring in this release, so they MUST NOT be exposed to operators. If the long-form lineage feature is picked up later it will arrive with first-class event emission, reducer ancestry handling, and replay fixtures; until then treat the contract below as canonical.
   531	
   532	| Mode | Session id | Generation | Archive | JSONL event | When to pick |
   533	|------|-----------|-----------|---------|-------------|--------------|
   534	| `new` | fresh | 1 | n/a | implicit (config record) | no existing state |
   535	| `resume` | same | same | none | `resumed` (see example above) | operator wants to continue the current lineage boundary |
   536	| `restart` | fresh | `prior + 1` | prior `review/` tree moved under `review_archive/{timestamp}/` | `restarted` (same field set plus non-null `archivedPath`) | operator wants to clear the workspace and replay with a new angle |
   537	
   538	**Contract for every persisted lifecycle event**:
   539	
   540	```json
   541	{
   542	  "type": "event",
   543	  "event": "resumed | restarted",
   544	  "mode": "review",
   545	  "sessionId": "<session id of the new or continuing lineage>",
   546	  "parentSessionId": "<session id of the prior lineage (equals sessionId on resume)>",
   547	  "lineageMode": "resume | restart",
   548	  "generation": <number>,
   549	  "continuedFromRun": <number or null>,
   550	  "archivedPath": "<path or null>",
   551	  "timestamp": "<ISO 8601>"
   552	}
   553	```
   554	
   555	Every field in the contract MUST be present on every persisted lifecycle event. `archivedPath` is null for `resumed` and set to the archive destination for `restarted`. `continuedFromRun` is the number of completed iteration records before the lifecycle boundary. Reducer parity tests ensure the dashboard `Lifecycle` section reads exactly those fields.
   556

   146	
   147	  lifecycleModes:
   148	    - id: new
   149	      description: Create a brand-new review lineage with generation 1 and no parent session.
   150	    - id: resume
   151	      description: Continue the current review lineage without changing generation.
   152	    - id: restart
   153	      description: Start a new generation for the same target and archive the prior review packet state.
   154	    - id: fork
   155	      description: Start a new review branch with parent linkage and full ancestry preserved.
   156	    - id: completed-continue
   157	      description: Snapshot the completed report, reopen the lineage, and continue with amendment-only deltas.
   158	
   159	  lineageFields:
   160	    - sessionId
   161	    - parentSessionId
   162	    - lineageMode
   163	    - generation
   164	    - continuedFromRun
   165

   167	    enabled: true
   168	    inputs:

   170	      - newIterationFile

   280	
   281	Failed gates persist `blockedStop` with full gate results in the journal.
   282	
   283	### Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)
   284	
   285	Sessions support a single lineage mode today: `new`. Every invocation of the `/improve:agent` workflow starts a fresh session with a new session id and generation 1. Multi-generation lineage modes (`resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`) were described in earlier drafts but have no shipped runtime wiring in the improve-agent workflow, reducer, or journal consumer.
   286	
   287	Operators who want to continue evaluating an agent after a prior session SHOULD archive the prior session folder (e.g. move `improve/` to `improve_archive/{timestamp}/`) and re-invoke the command, which starts a new `new`-mode session. The reducer treats each session independently and does not carry ancestry across sessions.
   288	
   289	If the long-form lineage feature is implemented later, it will arrive with first-class event emission in `improve_improve-agent_{auto,confirm}.yaml`, reducer ancestry handling in `sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs`, and replay fixtures. Until then, treat every session as a standalone evaluation.
   290	
   291	### Mutation Coverage Graph
   292	

 succeeded in 0ms:
   334	1. Read the prior journal from `{spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl`
   335	2. Replay journal state to determine `continuedFromIteration`
   336	3. Resume the iteration counter from that point
   337	4. Do NOT repeat already-completed iterations
   338	
   339	Supported lineage modes: `new`. sk-improve-agent is one-session-only in the current release — every invocation starts a fresh session with generation 1. `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no runtime wiring; see `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md §Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)` for the full retraction.
   340	
   341	### Step 7: Return Status
   342	
   343	- Completed normally: `STATUS=OK ITERATIONS={N} BEST_SCORE={score}`
   344	- User cancelled: `STATUS=CANCELLED`

 succeeded in 0ms:
    20	## 2. PARITY BASELINE
    21	
    22	Every runtime mirror for `@deep-research` must preserve these invariants:
    23	
    24	1. LEAF-only execution. No sub-agent dispatch.
    25	2. Canonical packet paths under `{spec_folder}/research/`, especially `research/deep-research-state.jsonl` and `research/findings-registry.json`.
    26	3. Lifecycle vocabulary: `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, `completed-continue`.
    27	4. Reducer-owned synchronization of strategy, dashboard, findings registry, and synthesis metadata.
    28	5. Canonical pause sentinel: `research/.deep-research-pause`.
    29	6. Lineage metadata keys: `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, `continuedFromRun`.
    30	
    31	If any mirror drifts on those invariants, treat it as a contract failure rather than a runtime-specific customization.
    32	
    33	---
    34	
    35	## 3. MATRIX
    36	
    37	| Runtime | Mirror Path | Tool Surface | Hook Bootstrap | Schema Adaptation | Fallback Bridge Eligible | Notes |
    38	|---------|-------------|--------------|----------------|-------------------|--------------------------|-------|
    39	| OpenCode / Copilot | `.opencode/agent/deep-research.md` | `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Bash`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `WebFetch`, memory | Yes (plugin) | Low | Yes | OpenCode uses plugin-based hooks (`@opencode-ai/plugin`); Copilot uses repo-local session-start banner hook. |
    40	| Claude | `.claude/agents/deep-research.md` | `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Bash`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `WebFetch`, MCP memory | Yes | Low | Yes | Default command YAML example path points here, but behavior is runtime-agnostic. |
    41	| Codex | `.codex/agents/deep-research.toml` | `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Bash`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `WebFetch`, memory MCP | Yes | Medium | Yes | TOML mirror must keep the same lifecycle/reducer contract as the Markdown mirrors. |
    42	| Gemini | `.gemini/agents/deep-research.md` | `read_file`, `write_file`, `replace`, `run_shell_command`, `grep_search`, `list_directory`, `google_web_search` | Yes | High | Yes | Tool names differ, but packet contract and lifecycle semantics must not. |
    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	## 4. ADAPTATION RULES
    47	
    48	Allowed runtime adaptation:
    49	
    50	- Tool names and transport details may change per runtime.
    51	- Provider-specific request formatting may change.
    52	- Hook-based startup ergonomics may change.
    53	
    54	Forbidden runtime drift:
    55	
    56	- Renaming packet artifacts.
    57	- Changing lifecycle branch meaning.
    58	- Letting agent mirrors own reducer outputs.
    59	- Treating logs or traces as the source of truth instead of packet files.
    60	
    61	---
    62	
    63	## 5. NON-HOOK EQUIVALENCE
    64	
    65	Hook-capable and non-hook runtimes must reach the same branch decision when given the same packet files:
    66	
    67	- Active packet -> `resume`
    68	- Completed packet reopened -> `completed-continue`
    69	- Explicit user reset -> `restart`
    70	- Explicit branch-from-current-state -> `fork`
    71	
    72	The packet, not the hook state, decides the branch.

 succeeded in 0ms:
    90	
    91	### Lifecycle Branch Contract (current release)
    92	
    93	The runtime supports three lineage modes today. `fork` and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no workflow wiring in this release, so they MUST NOT be exposed to operators. If the long-form lineage feature is picked up later it will arrive with first-class event emission, reducer ancestry handling, and replay fixtures; until then treat the contract below as canonical.
    94	
    95	| Mode | Session id | Generation | Archive | JSONL event | When to pick |
    96	|------|-----------|-----------|---------|-------------|--------------|
    97	| `new` | fresh | 1 | n/a | implicit (config record) | no existing state |
    98	| `resume` | same | same | none | `resumed` (see example above) | operator wants to continue the current lineage boundary |
    99	| `restart` | fresh | `prior + 1` | prior `research/` tree moved under `research_archive/{timestamp}/` | `restarted` (same field set plus `archivedPath`) | operator wants to clear the workspace and replay with a new angle |
   100	
   101	**Contract for every persisted lifecycle event**:
   102	
   103	```json
   104	{
   105	  "type": "event",
   106	  "event": "resumed | restarted",
   107	  "mode": "research",
   108	  "sessionId": "<session id of the new or continuing lineage>",
   109	  "parentSessionId": "<session id of the prior lineage (equals sessionId on resume)>",
   110	  "lineageMode": "resume | restart",
   111	  "generation": <number>,
   112	  "continuedFromRun": <number or null>,
   113	  "archivedPath": "<path or null>",
   114	  "timestamp": "<ISO 8601>"
   115	}
   116	```
   117	
   118	Every field in the contract MUST be present on every persisted lifecycle event. `archivedPath` is null for `resumed` and set to the archive destination for `restarted`. `continuedFromRun` is the number of completed iteration records before the lifecycle boundary. Reducer parity tests ensure the dashboard `Lifecycle` section reads exactly those fields.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   220	
   221	The optional `graphEvents` array records coverage graph mutations emitted by a review iteration. The MCP coverage graph handlers (`mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts`) consume these events and persist them into `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`, where they become the source of truth for graph-assisted convergence, hotspot saturation, and blocked-stop evidence.
   222	
   223	```json
   224	{
   225	  "type": "iteration", "mode": "review", "run": 4, "status": "complete",
   226	  "focus": "correctness",
   227	  "dimensions": ["correctness"],
   228	  "filesReviewed": ["src/api/session.ts"],
   229	  "findingsSummary": { "P0": 0, "P1": 1, "P2": 0 },
   230	  "findingsNew": { "P0": 0, "P1": 1, "P2": 0 },
   231	  "newFindingsRatio": 0.18,
   232	  "sessionId": "rvw-2026-03-24T10-00-00Z",
   233	  "generation": 1,
   234	  "lineageMode": "new",
   235	  "graphEvents": [
   236	    { "type": "dimension", "id": "d-correctness", "label": "correctness" },
   237	    { "type": "file", "id": "file-session-ts", "label": "src/api/session.ts" },
   238	    { "type": "finding", "id": "f-001", "label": "Missing CSRF token check on session POST" },
   239	    { "type": "evidence", "id": "ev-001", "label": "src/api/session.ts:42" },
   240	    { "type": "edge", "id": "e-in-dim-f-001", "relation": "IN_DIMENSION", "source": "f-001", "target": "d-correctness" },
   241	    { "type": "edge", "id": "e-in-file-f-001", "relation": "IN_FILE", "source": "f-001", "target": "file-session-ts" },
   242	    { "type": "edge", "id": "e-evidence-f-001", "relation": "EVIDENCE_FOR", "source": "ev-001", "target": "f-001" }
   243	  ],
   244	  "timestamp": "2026-03-24T10:12:00Z",
   245	  "durationMs": 51000
   246	}
   247	```
   248	
   249	#### Event payload shape
   250	
   251	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   252	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
   253	| type | `"dimension"` \| `"file"` \| `"finding"` \| `"evidence"` \| `"remediation"` \| `"edge"` | Yes | Review-loop node kind or the literal `"edge"` for relation rows |
   254	| id | string | Yes | Caller-chosen logical id. Must be unique **within** a single `(specFolder, loopType, sessionId)` namespace; see Namespace Rules below |
   255	| label | string | Yes | Human-readable name/caption (dimension, path, finding title, evidence anchor, etc.) |
   256	| relation | `"COVERS"` \| `"EVIDENCE_FOR"` \| `"CONTRADICTS"` \| `"RESOLVES"` \| `"CONFIRMS"` \| `"ESCALATES"` \| `"IN_DIMENSION"` \| `"IN_FILE"` | edges only | Review relation type. Required when `type == "edge"` |
   257	| source | string | edges only | `id` of the source node inside the current namespace |
   258	| target | string | edges only | `id` of the target node inside the current namespace. Self-loops are rejected by the upsert handler |
   259	| metadata | object | No | Optional free-form metadata persisted alongside the row (e.g. `{ "hotspot_score": 2 }`) |
   260	
   261	#### Namespace Rules (REQ-028, REQ-029)
   262	
   263	`graphEvents` entries are scoped by the session that emits them. The coverage graph DB uses a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)`, so two independent review sessions in the same spec folder MAY reuse the same logical `id` without collision — each session gets its own row.
   264	
   265	Concrete obligations for producers:

 succeeded in 0ms:
   220	### Graph Events
   221	
   222	The optional `graphEvents` array records the coverage graph mutations an iteration emits. The MCP coverage graph handlers (`mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts`) consume these events and persist them into `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` where they become the source of truth for graph-assisted convergence.
   223	
   224	```json
   225	{"type":"iteration","run":4,"status":"complete","focus":"Reconnection strategies","findingsCount":3,"newInfoRatio":0.55,"graphEvents":[{"type":"question","id":"q-reconnect","label":"How should the client retry after a disconnect?"},{"type":"finding","id":"f-backoff","label":"Exponential backoff converges under jitter"},{"type":"edge","id":"e-answers-reconnect","relation":"ANSWERS","source":"f-backoff","target":"q-reconnect"}],"keyQuestions":["How should the client retry after a disconnect?"],"answeredQuestions":[],"timestamp":"2026-03-18T10:15:00Z","durationMs":48000}
   226	```
   227	
   228	#### Event payload shape
   229	
   230	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   231	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
   232	| type | `"question"` \| `"finding"` \| `"claim"` \| `"source"` \| `"edge"` | Yes | Research-loop node kind or the literal `"edge"` for relation rows |
   233	| id | string | Yes | Caller-chosen logical id. Must be unique **within** a single `(specFolder, loopType, sessionId)` namespace; see Namespace Rules below |
   234	| label | string | Yes | Human-readable name/caption for dashboards and synthesis |
   235	| relation | `"ANSWERS"` \| `"SUPPORTS"` \| `"CONTRADICTS"` \| `"SUPERSEDES"` \| `"DERIVED_FROM"` \| `"COVERS"` \| `"CITES"` | edges only | Research relation type. Required when `type == "edge"` |
   236	| source | string | edges only | `id` of the source node inside the current namespace |
   237	| target | string | edges only | `id` of the target node inside the current namespace. Self-loops are rejected by the upsert handler |
   238	| metadata | object | No | Optional free-form metadata persisted alongside the row |
   239	
   240	#### Namespace Rules (REQ-028, REQ-029)
   241	
   242	`graphEvents` entries are scoped by the session that emits them. The coverage graph DB uses a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)`, so two independent sessions in the same spec folder MAY reuse the same logical `id` without collision — each session gets an independent row.
   243	
   244	Concrete obligations for producers:
   245	

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "DRV-015 -- Review iterations emit structured graphEvents"
     3	description: "Verify that review iteration records carry a graphEvents array with dimension_node, file_node, and finding_node entries."
     4	---
     5	
     6	# DRV-015 -- Review iterations emit structured graphEvents
     7	
     8	This document captures the realistic user-testing contract, current behavior, execution flow, source anchors, and metadata for `DRV-015`.
     9	
    10	---
    11	
    12	## 1. OVERVIEW
    13	
    14	This scenario validates structured graph event emission for `DRV-015`. The objective is to verify that a running deep review iteration writes a `graphEvents` array that includes review graph nodes such as `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node`.
    15	
    16	### WHY THIS MATTERS
    17	
    18	Graph-aware review convergence depends on replayable iteration-level graph data. If review iterations do not persist structured graph events, graph-assisted coverage analysis cannot confirm which dimensions touched which files or whether findings are connected to evidence.
    19	
    20	---
    21	
    22	## 2. CURRENT REALITY
    23	
    24	Operators should run this as a real orchestrator-led check rather than a synthetic command-matrix exercise. The scenario is only complete when the operator can explain the behavior back to a user in plain language.
    25	
    26	- Title: Review iterations emit structured graphEvents.
    27	- Given: A running deep review iteration.
    28	- When: The iteration completes with P0/P1/P2 findings.
    29	- Then: The JSONL record contains `graphEvents` with `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node` entries.
    30	- Real user request: When a review iteration finishes, what graph data is written so coverage and convergence can replay the review state?
    31	- Orchestrator prompt: Validate the structured `graphEvents` contract for sk-deep-review. Confirm that graph-aware review convergence expects `graphEvents` in iteration records, and that graph replay tests show review JSONL records carrying `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node` entries, then return a concise operator-facing verdict.
    32	- Expected execution process: Inspect the deep-review convergence reference for the graph-aware iteration-record contract first, then the coverage-graph replay tests for concrete review node types and JSONL-shaped examples.
    33	- Desired user-facing outcome: The user understands that completed review iterations emit replayable graph events and which review node types are expected in those records.
    34	- Expected signals: `graphEvents` referenced as iteration-record input for graph-aware review convergence; replay tests include review node-type coverage for `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node`.
    35	- Pass/fail posture: PASS if the convergence reference and graph replay tests agree that completed review iterations carry `graphEvents` and that review node types include `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node`; FAIL if the record contract is absent or replay coverage is missing.
    36	
    37	---
    38	
    39	## 3. TEST EXECUTION
    40	
    41	### RECOMMENDED ORCHESTRATION PROCESS
    42	
    43	1. Restate the user request in plain language before inspecting implementation details.
    44	2. Follow the listed command sequence in order so higher-level docs are checked before lower-level test contracts.
    45	3. Capture evidence that would let another operator reproduce the verdict without re-deriving the scenario.
    46	4. Return a short user-facing explanation, not just raw implementation notes.
    47	
    48	| Feature ID | Feature Name | Scenario Name / Objective | Exact Prompt | Exact Command Sequence | Expected Signals | Evidence | Pass/Fail Criteria | Failure Triage |
    49	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    50	| DRV-015 | Review iterations emit structured graphEvents | Verify completed review iterations emit `graphEvents` with `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node` coverage. | Validate the structured `graphEvents` contract for sk-deep-review. Confirm that graph-aware review convergence expects `graphEvents` in iteration records, and that the graph replay tests show review JSONL records carrying `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node` entries, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: rg -n 'graphEvents|review iteration records|graph-aware review convergence' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md` -> 2. `bash: rg -n 'graphEvents|dimension_node|file_node|finding_node|reviewNodeTypes' .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts` -> 3. `bash: rg -n 'graphEvents|finding_node' .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-convergence.vitest.ts` | `graphEvents` used as iteration-record input; replay tests show review node-type coverage including `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node`. | Capture the convergence reference lines that describe `graphEvents` in review iteration records, the review node-type list, and one replay example showing JSONL-shaped `graphEvents`. | PASS if the convergence reference and replay tests agree that completed review iterations emit `graphEvents` and that review graph node coverage includes `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node`; FAIL if any of those pieces are missing or contradictory. | Privilege the convergence reference for the contract and the replay tests for concrete review node-type evidence. |
    51	
    52	---
    53	
    54	## 4. SOURCE FILES
    55	
    56	### PLAYBOOK SOURCES
    57

    67	| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts` | Coverage-graph replay tests; review node-type coverage includes `dimension_node`, `file_node`, and `finding_node` |

     1	---
     2	title: "DR-029 -- Research iterations emit structured graphEvents"
     3	description: "Verify that research iteration records carry a graphEvents array with question_node, finding_node, and source_node entries."
     4	---
     5	
     6	# DR-029 -- Research iterations emit structured graphEvents
     7	
     8	This document captures the realistic user-testing contract, current behavior, execution flow, source anchors, and metadata for `DR-029`.
     9	
    10	---
    11	
    12	## 1. OVERVIEW
    13	
    14	This scenario validates structured graph event emission for `DR-029`. The objective is to verify that a running deep research iteration writes a `graphEvents` array that includes research graph nodes such as `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node`.
    15	
    16	### WHY THIS MATTERS
    17	
    18	Graph-aware convergence depends on replayable graph data from iteration records. If research iterations do not persist structured graph events, reducers and graph tooling cannot reconstruct question-to-finding-to-source coverage, and graph-aware stop analysis degrades to missing-data behavior.
    19	
    20	---
    21	
    22	## 2. CURRENT REALITY
    23	
    24	Operators should run this as a real orchestrator-led check rather than a synthetic command-matrix exercise. The scenario is only complete when the operator can explain the behavior back to a user in plain language.
    25	
    26	- Title: Research iterations emit structured graphEvents.
    27	- Given: A running deep research iteration.
    28	- When: The iteration completes with findings.
    29	- Then: The JSONL record contains a `graphEvents` array with `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node` entries.
    30	- Real user request: When a research iteration finishes, what graph data gets written into state so convergence and tooling can replay it later?
    31	- Orchestrator prompt: Validate the structured `graphEvents` contract for sk-deep-research. Confirm that graph-aware research convergence expects `graphEvents` in iteration records, and that the graph replay tests show research JSONL records carrying `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node` entries, then return a concise operator-facing verdict.
    32	- Expected execution process: Inspect the deep-research convergence reference for the graph-aware iteration-record contract first, then the coverage-graph replay tests for concrete research node types and JSONL-shaped examples.
    33	- Desired user-facing outcome: The user understands that completed research iterations emit replayable graph events and which research node types are expected in those records.
    34	- Expected signals: `graphEvents` referenced as iteration-record input for graph-aware convergence; replay tests include `kind: "node"` entries for `question_node`, `finding_node`, and research node-type coverage including `source_node`.
    35	- Pass/fail posture: PASS if the convergence reference and graph replay tests agree that completed research iterations carry `graphEvents` and that research node types include `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node`; FAIL if the record contract is absent or the replay tests do not cover those node types.
    36	
    37	---
    38	
    39	## 3. TEST EXECUTION
    40	
    41	### RECOMMENDED ORCHESTRATION PROCESS
    42	
    43	1. Restate the user request in plain language before inspecting implementation details.
    44	2. Follow the listed command sequence in order so higher-level docs are checked before lower-level test contracts.
    45	3. Capture evidence that would let another operator reproduce the verdict without re-deriving the scenario.
    46	4. Return a short user-facing explanation, not just raw implementation notes.
    47	
    48	| Feature ID | Feature Name | Scenario Name / Objective | Exact Prompt | Exact Command Sequence | Expected Signals | Evidence | Pass/Fail Criteria | Failure Triage |
    49	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    50	| DR-029 | Research iterations emit structured graphEvents | Verify completed research iterations emit `graphEvents` with `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node` coverage. | Validate the structured `graphEvents` contract for sk-deep-research. Confirm that graph-aware research convergence expects `graphEvents` in iteration records, and that the graph replay tests show research JSONL records carrying `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node` entries, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: rg -n 'graphEvents|iteration records|graph-aware convergence' .opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md` -> 2. `bash: rg -n 'graphEvents|question_node|finding_node' .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-convergence.vitest.ts` -> 3. `bash: rg -n 'graphEvents|researchNodeTypes|question_node|finding_node|source_node' .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts` | `graphEvents` used as iteration-record input; replay tests show `question_node` and `finding_node` entries; research node-type coverage includes `source_node`. | Capture the convergence reference lines that describe `graphEvents` in iteration records, a replay-test record with `graphEvents`, and the research node-type list that includes `source_node`. | PASS if the convergence reference and replay tests agree that completed research iterations emit `graphEvents` and that research graph node coverage includes `question_node`, `finding_node`, and `source_node`; FAIL if any of those pieces are missing or contradictory. | Privilege the convergence reference for the contract and the replay tests for concrete JSONL-shaped evidence. If the node-type list and replay examples diverge, treat the tests as implementation truth and flag doc drift. |

     4	// Tests for the coverage graph database projection contract.

     7	
     8	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
     9	
    10	/**
    11	 * Coverage Graph DB contract test stubs.
    12	 *
    13	 * These tests validate the contract defined in state_format.md
    14	 * Section 12 (Coverage Graph Integration Contract). The actual
    15	 * MCP implementation will be wired in Phase 3; these tests
    16	 * verify the contract shape and expected behaviors.
    17	 */
    18	
    19	// --- Contract shape validation ---
    20	
    21	interface UpsertPayload {
    22	  namespace: string;
    23	  nodes: Array<{ id: string; type: string; label: string; iteration: number }>;
    24	  edges: Array<{ source: string; target: string; relation: string; weight: number; iteration: number }>;
    25	  iteration: number;
    26	}
    27	
    28	interface UpsertResponse {
    29	  accepted: number;
    30	  rejected: number;
    31	  signals: {
    32	    componentCount: number;
    33	    largestComponentSize: number;
    34	    isolatedNodes: number;
    35	    totalNodes: number;
    36	    totalEdges: number;
    37	  };
    38	}
    39	
    40	describe('coverage-graph-db contract', () => {
    41	  describe('upsert payload shape', () => {
    42	    it('validates a well-formed upsert payload', () => {
    43	      const payload: UpsertPayload = {
    44	        namespace: 'dr-2026-03-18T10-00-00Z',
    45	        nodes: [
    46	          { id: 'q-1', type: 'question_node', label: 'What causes latency?', iteration: 3 },
    47	          { id: 'f-2', type: 'finding_node', label: 'Connection pooling bottleneck', iteration: 3 },
    48	        ],
    49	        edges: [
    50	          { source: 'f-2', target: 'q-1', relation: 'ANSWERS', weight: 1.3, iteration: 3 },
    51	        ],
    52	        iteration: 3,
    53	      };
    54	
    55	      expect(payload.namespace).toBeTruthy();
    56	      expect(payload.nodes.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    57	      expect(payload.edges.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    58	      expect(payload.iteration).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    59	    });
    60	
    61	    it('requires namespace on every payload', () => {
    62	      const payload: UpsertPayload = {
    63	        namespace: '',
    64	        nodes: [],
    65	        edges: [],
    66	        iteration: 1,
    67	      };
    68	
    69	      // Empty namespace should be rejected
    70	      expect(payload.namespace).toBeFalsy();
    71	    });
    72	
    73	    it('accepts empty nodes and edges arrays', () => {
    74	      const payload: UpsertPayload = {
    75	        namespace: 'test-ns',
    76	        nodes: [],
    77	        edges: [],
    78	        iteration: 1,
    79	      };
    80	
    81	      expect(payload.nodes.length).toBe(0);
    82	      expect(payload.edges.length).toBe(0);
    83	    });
    84	  });
    85	
    86	  describe('upsert response shape', () => {
    87	    it('validates a well-formed response', () => {
    88	      const response: UpsertResponse = {
    89	        accepted: 5,
    90	        rejected: 0,
    91	        signals: {
    92	          componentCount: 2,
    93	          largestComponentSize: 8,
    94	          isolatedNodes: 1,
    95	          totalNodes: 10,
    96	          totalEdges: 12,
    97	        },
    98	      };
    99	
   100	      expect(response.accepted).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   101	      expect(response.rejected).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   102	      expect(response.accepted + response.rejected).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   103	      expect(response.signals.componentCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   104	      expect(response.signals.totalNodes).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   105	    });
   106	
   107	    it('signals contain all required fields', () => {
   108	      const signals: UpsertResponse['signals'] = {
   109	        componentCount: 1,
   110	        largestComponentSize: 5,
   111	        isolatedNodes: 0,
   112	        totalNodes: 5,
   113	        totalEdges: 4,
   114	      };
   115	
   116	      const requiredKeys = ['componentCount', 'largestComponentSize', 'isolatedNodes', 'totalNodes', 'totalEdges'];
   117	      for (const key of requiredKeys) {
   118	        expect(signals).toHaveProperty(key);
   119	      }
   120	    });
   121	  });
   122	
   123	  describe('latency budget contract', () => {
   124	    it('defines 500ms budget for upsert batches', () => {
   125	      const UPSERT_BUDGET_MS = 500;
   126	      expect(UPSERT_BUDGET_MS).toBe(500);
   127	    });
   128	
   129	    it('defines 200ms budget for queries', () => {
   130	      const QUERY_BUDGET_MS = 200;
   131	      expect(QUERY_BUDGET_MS).toBe(200);
   132	    });
   133	  });
   134	
   135	  describe('replay semantics', () => {
   136	    it('graph is rebuildable from JSONL alone', () => {
   137	      // Simulate replay: collect all graphEvents from iteration records
   138	      const iterationRecords = [
   139	        {
   140	          type: 'iteration',
   141	          run: 1,
   142	          graphEvents: [
   143	            { kind: 'node', id: 'q-1', nodeType: 'question_node', label: 'Q1' },
   144	            { kind: 'edge', source: 'f-1', target: 'q-1', relation: 'ANSWERS' },
   145	          ],
   146	        },
   147	        {
   148	          type: 'iteration',
   149	          run: 2,
   150	          graphEvents: [
   151	            { kind: 'node', id: 'f-2', nodeType: 'finding_node', label: 'F2' },
   152	            { kind: 'edge', source: 'f-2', target: 'q-1', relation: 'ANSWERS' },
   153	          ],
   154	        },
   155	      ];
   156	
   157	      // Replay: collect all events
   158	      const allNodes: Array<{ kind: string; id: string }> = [];
   159	      const allEdges: Array<{ kind: string; source: string; target: string }> = [];
   160	
   161	      for (const record of iterationRecords) {
   162	        for (const event of record.graphEvents) {
   163	          if (event.kind === 'node') allNodes.push(event as { kind: string; id: string });
   164	          if (event.kind === 'edge') allEdges.push(event as { kind: string; source: string; target: string });
   165	        }
   166	      }
   167	
   168	      expect(allNodes.length).toBe(2);
   169	      expect(allEdges.length).toBe(2);
   170	    });
   171	
   172	    it('replay produces deterministic results', () => {
   173	      const events = [
   174	        { kind: 'node', id: 'a', nodeType: 'finding_node', label: 'A' },
   175	        { kind: 'node', id: 'b', nodeType: 'finding_node', label: 'B' },
   176	        { kind: 'edge', source: 'a', target: 'b', relation: 'CITES' },
   177	      ];
   178	
   179	      // Two replays of the same events should produce the same graph
   180	      const replay1Nodes = events.filter((e) => e.kind === 'node');

   182

   200	  });
   201	
   202	  describe('node type taxonomy', () => {

   228	
   229	    it('review relations are well-defined', () => {

   749	
   750	  return `## Sample Quality
   751	
   752	| Field | Value |
   753	| --- | --- |
   754	| replayCount | ${formatDashboardValue(sampleQuality.replayCount)} |
   755	| stabilityCoefficient | ${formatDashboardValue(sampleQuality.stabilityCoefficient)} |
   756	| insufficientSampleIterations | ${sampleQuality.insufficientSampleIterations} |
   757	| insufficientDataIterations | ${sampleQuality.insufficientDataIterations} |
   758	${explanation}`;

   760	
   761	function renderDashboard(registry, sampleQuality) {
   762	  const sections = Object.values(registry.profiles)
   763	    .sort((left, right) => left.profileId.localeCompare(right.profileId))
   764	    .map((bucket) => renderProfileSection(bucket))
   765	    .join('\n');
   766	
   767	  let recommendation = 'Continue only when the next run has a clearer signal than the current best-known state.';
   768	  if (registry.stopStatus?.shouldStop) {
   769	    recommendation = `Stop automatically: ${registry.stopStatus.reasons.join('; ')}`;
   770	  } else if (registry.globalMetrics.infraFailureCount > 0) {
   771	    recommendation = 'Stabilize infrastructure before trusting further comparisons.';
   772	  } else if (registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkFailCount > 0) {
   773	    recommendation = 'Fix repeated benchmark failures before broadening scope or promoting any target.';
   774	  }
   775	
   776	  return `# Agent Improvement Dashboard
   777	
   778	## Global Summary
   779	
   780	| Field | Value |
   781	| --- | --- |
   782	| Total records | ${registry.globalMetrics.totalRecords} |
   783	| Target profiles | ${registry.globalMetrics.targetProfiles} |
   784	| Prompt runs | ${registry.globalMetrics.promptRuns} |
   785	| Benchmark runs | ${registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkRuns} |
   786	| Accepted candidates | ${registry.globalMetrics.acceptedCount} |
   787	| Rejected candidates | ${registry.globalMetrics.rejectedCount} |
   788	| Ties | ${registry.globalMetrics.tieCount} |
   789	| Keep-baseline results | ${registry.globalMetrics.keepBaselineCount} |
   790	| Benchmark passes | ${registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkPassCount} |
   791	| Benchmark fails | ${registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkFailCount} |
   792	| Infra failures | ${registry.globalMetrics.infraFailureCount} |
   793	
   794	${renderSampleQualitySection(sampleQuality)}
   795	
   796	## Guardrails
   797	
   798	- All targets evaluated via dynamic mode; promotion requires explicit per-target approval
   799	- Mirror sync stays downstream packaging and is not counted as benchmark truth
   800	
   801	## Stop Status
   802	
   803	- Should stop: ${registry.stopStatus?.shouldStop ? 'yes' : 'no'}
   804	- Drift ambiguity: ${registry.stopStatus?.driftAmbiguity ? 'yes' : 'no'}
   805	- Reasons: ${registry.stopStatus?.reasons?.length ? registry.stopStatus.reasons.join('; ') : 'none'}
   806	
   807	${sections}
   808	
   809	## Recommendation
   810	
   811	${recommendation}
   812	`;
   813	}

   818	
   819	function main() {
   820	  const runtimeRoot = process.argv[2];
   821	  if (!runtimeRoot) {
   822	    process.stderr.write('Usage: node reduce-state.cjs <improvement-runtime-root>\n');
   823	    process.exit(2);
   824	  }
   825	
   826	  const stateLogPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'agent-improvement-state.jsonl');
   827	  const registryPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'experiment-registry.json');
   828	  const dashboardPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'agent-improvement-dashboard.md');
   829	  const configPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'agent-improvement-config.json');
   830	  const mirrorDriftReportPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'mirror-drift-report.md');
   831	  const journalPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'improvement-journal.jsonl');
   832	  const candidateLineagePath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'candidate-lineage.json');
   833	  const mutationCoveragePath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'mutation-coverage.json');
   834	
   835	  const records = parseJsonl(readUtf8(stateLogPath));
   836	  const registry = buildRegistry(records);
   837	  registry.journalSummary = buildJournalSummary(journalPath);
   838	  registry.candidateLineage = buildCandidateLineageSummary(candidateLineagePath);
   839	  registry.mutationCoverage = buildMutationCoverageSummary(mutationCoveragePath);
   840	  const config = parseJson(readOptionalUtf8(configPath) || '{}', {});
   841	  const mirrorDriftReport = readOptionalUtf8(mirrorDriftReportPath);
   842	  registry.stopStatus = evaluateStopStatus(registry, config, mirrorDriftReport);
   843	  const sampleQuality = summarizeSampleQuality(records, registry);
   844	  const dashboard = renderDashboard(registry, sampleQuality);
   845	
   846	  writeUtf8(registryPath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\n`);
   847	  writeUtf8(dashboardPath, dashboard);
   848	}
   849	
   850	main();

     1	---
     2	title: "Replay Consumer Artifact Verification"
     3	feature_id: "RT-034"
     4	category: "Runtime Truth"
     5	---
     6	
     7	# Replay Consumer Artifact Verification
     8	
     9	Validates ADR-002 Option A replay-consumer behavior: `reduce-state.cjs` reads `improvement-journal.jsonl`, `candidate-lineage.json`, and `mutation-coverage.json`, writes their summaries into the registry, and degrades gracefully when any one artifact is missing.
    10	
    11	Given: an improvement runtime where `improvement-journal.jsonl`, `candidate-lineage.json`, and `mutation-coverage.json` are all present.
    12	When: the operator runs `reduce-state.cjs` and then repeats the run with one artifact removed at a time from a disposable runtime copy.
    13	Then: the registry contains `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage`; the dashboard renders each as a distinct replay-consumer section; and any missing artifact resolves to `null` without throwing.
    14	
    15	## Prompt / Command
    16	
    17	```text
    18	node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec}/improvement
    19	```
    20	
    21	### Verification (copy-paste)
    22	
    23	```bash
    24	RUNTIME_COPY="$(mktemp -d /tmp/improve-agent-replay-XXXXXX)"
    25	cp -R "{spec}/improvement/." "$RUNTIME_COPY/"
    26	
    27	node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs "$RUNTIME_COPY"
    28	
    29	cat "$RUNTIME_COPY/experiment-registry.json" | jq '.journalSummary, .candidateLineage, .mutationCoverage'
    30	grep -n "Journal Summary\|Candidate Lineage\|Mutation Coverage" "$RUNTIME_COPY/agent-improvement-dashboard.md"
    31	
    32	python3 - <<'PY' "$RUNTIME_COPY/experiment-registry.json"
    33	import json, sys
    34	registry = json.load(open(sys.argv[1], 'r', encoding='utf-8'))
    35	js = registry.get('journalSummary')
    36	cl = registry.get('candidateLineage')
    37	mc = registry.get('mutationCoverage')
    38	assert js is not None, 'journalSummary missing'
    39	assert cl is not None, 'candidateLineage missing'
    40	assert mc is not None, 'mutationCoverage missing'
    41	required_js = ['lastSessionStart', 'lastSessionEnd', 'totalEvents', 'eventTypeCounts', 'stopReason', 'sessionOutcome']
    42	required_cl = ['lineageDepth', 'totalCandidates', 'currentLeaf']
    43	required_mc = ['coverageRatio', 'uncoveredMutations']
    44	for key in required_js:
    45	    assert key in js, f'journalSummary missing {key}'
    46	for key in required_cl:
    47	    assert key in cl, f'candidateLineage missing {key}'
    48	for key in required_mc:
    49	    assert key in mc, f'mutationCoverage missing {key}'
    50	print('PASS — reducer populated all three replay consumer summaries')
    51	PY
    52	
    53	for artifact in improvement-journal.jsonl candidate-lineage.json mutation-coverage.json; do
    54	  TMP_CASE="$(mktemp -d /tmp/improve-agent-replay-case-XXXXXX)"
    55	  cp -R "$RUNTIME_COPY/." "$TMP_CASE/"
    56	  rm -f "$TMP_CASE/$artifact"
    57	  node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs "$TMP_CASE"
    58	  case "$artifact" in
    59	    improvement-journal.jsonl)
    60	      cat "$TMP_CASE/experiment-registry.json" | jq '.journalSummary'
    61	      test "$(cat "$TMP_CASE/experiment-registry.json" | jq -r '.journalSummary')" = "null"
    62	      ;;
    63	    candidate-lineage.json)
    64	      cat "$TMP_CASE/experiment-registry.json" | jq '.candidateLineage'
    65	      test "$(cat "$TMP_CASE/experiment-registry.json" | jq -r '.candidateLineage')" = "null"
    66	      ;;
    67	    mutation-coverage.json)
    68	      cat "$TMP_CASE/experiment-registry.json" | jq '.mutationCoverage'
    69	      test "$(cat "$TMP_CASE/experiment-registry.json" | jq -r '.mutationCoverage')" = "null"
    70	      ;;
    71	  esac
    72	  rm -rf "$TMP_CASE"
    73	done
    74	
    75	rm -rf "$RUNTIME_COPY"
    76	```
    77	
    78	## Expected Signals
    79	
    80	- `experiment-registry.json` contains:
    81	  - `journalSummary` with `lastSessionStart`, `lastSessionEnd`, `totalEvents`, `eventTypeCounts`, `stopReason`, `sessionOutcome`
    82	  - `candidateLineage` with `lineageDepth`, `totalCandidates`, `currentLeaf`
    83	  - `mutationCoverage` with `coverageRatio`, `uncoveredMutations`
    84	- `agent-improvement-dashboard.md` renders replay-consumer output as distinct sections for journal summary, candidate lineage, and mutation coverage
    85	- Re-running the reducer with any one of the 3 artifacts removed does not throw
    86	- The corresponding registry field resolves to `null` when its source artifact is missing
    87	- The remaining consumer paths still populate normally when only one artifact is absent
    88	
    89	## Pass Criteria
    90	
    91	With all three replay artifacts present, `reduce-state.cjs` populates `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage` in the registry and the dashboard exposes each consumer as a separate section. When any one artifact is missing, the reducer completes successfully and sets only the corresponding field to `null` while preserving the others.
    92	
    93	## Failure Triage
    94	
    95	- If any registry summary is missing with artifacts present: inspect `buildJournalSummary()`, `buildCandidateLineageSummary()`, or `buildMutationCoverageSummary()` in `reduce-state.cjs`
    96	- If the reducer throws when one artifact is removed: verify `readOptionalUtf8()` / `readOptionalJson()` are still used for graceful degradation
    97	- If a missing artifact silently drops multiple fields: confirm each summary builder is wired independently from its own path
    98	- If the dashboard does not render the replay consumer summaries: add explicit dashboard sections for journal summary, candidate lineage, and mutation coverage instead of leaving them registry-only
    99	
   100	## Evidence Template
   101	
   102	```text
   103	Verdict: [PASS/FAIL]
   104	Date: [YYYY-MM-DD]
   105	Tester: [name]
   106	Output excerpt:
   107	[paste jq output for journalSummary / candidateLineage / mutationCoverage and one null-on-missing rerun]
   108	```

 succeeded in 0ms:
   324	**sessionOutcome** (WHAT):
   325	- `keptBaseline` — Baseline retained
   326	- `promoted` — Candidate promoted to canonical target
   327	- `rolledBack` — Promotion reversed
   328	- `advisoryOnly` — Assessment only, no mutation
   329	
   330	### Step 6D: Resume Semantics (Phase 005)
   331	
   332	When `--session-id=<prior-id>` is provided:
   333	
   334	1. Read the prior journal from `{spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl`
   335	2. Replay journal state to determine `continuedFromIteration`
   336	3. Resume the iteration counter from that point
   337	4. Do NOT repeat already-completed iterations
   338	
   339	Supported lineage modes: `new`. sk-improve-agent is one-session-only in the current release — every invocation starts a fresh session with generation 1. `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue` were described in earlier drafts but have no runtime wiring; see `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md §Resume/Continuation Semantics (current release)` for the full retraction.

---
description: "Evaluate and improve any agent across 5 integration-aware dimensions with proposal-first candidates, deterministic scoring, and guarded promotion — supports :auto and :confirm modes"
argument-hint: "<agent_path> [:auto|:confirm] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--iterations=N]"
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task
---

> **EXECUTION PROTOCOL — READ FIRST**
>
> This command runs a structured workflow. Do NOT dispatch agents from this document.
>
> **YOUR FIRST ACTION:**
> 1. Run Phase 0: @general agent self-verification (below)
> 2. Run Setup Phase: consolidated prompt to gather inputs
> 3. Determine execution mode from user input (`:auto` or `:confirm`)
> 4. Load matching YAML workflow and execute
>
> This command is **general-agent based** and does **not** require `@write`.

---

# 🚨 PHASE 0: @GENERAL AGENT VERIFICATION

**STATUS: ☐ BLOCKED**

```
EXECUTE THIS AUTOMATIC SELF-CHECK (NOT A USER QUESTION):

SELF-CHECK: Are you operating as the @general agent?
│
├─ INDICATORS that you ARE @general agent:
│   ├─ You were invoked without @write-only constraints
│   ├─ You can orchestrate Read/Write/Edit/Bash workflow execution
│   ├─ You can load skill references and execute defined logic
│
├─ IF YES (all indicators present):
│   └─ general_agent_verified = TRUE → Continue to Setup Phase
│
└─ IF NO or UNCERTAIN:
    │
    ├─ ⛔ HARD BLOCK - DO NOT PROCEED
    │
    ├─ DISPLAY to user:
    │   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │   │ ⛔ GENERAL AGENT REQUIRED                                  │
    │   │                                                            │
    │   │ This command orchestrates sk-improve-agent skill          │
    │   │ invocation and does not require @write routing.            │
    │   │                                                            │
    │   │ To proceed, restart with:                                  │
    │   │   /improve:agent [arguments]                               │
    │   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    │
    └─ RETURN: STATUS=FAIL ERROR="General agent required"

# 🔒 UNIFIED SETUP PHASE

**STATUS: ☐ BLOCKED**

**🚨 SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT - ONE USER INTERACTION**

This workflow uses a SINGLE consolidated prompt to gather ALL required inputs in ONE user interaction.

**Round-trip optimization:** This workflow requires only 1 user interaction for setup.

```
EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:

1. CHECK for mode suffix in $ARGUMENTS or command invocation:
   ├─ ":auto" suffix detected → execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS" (pre-set, omit Q2)
   ├─ ":confirm" suffix detected → execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE" (pre-set, omit Q2)
   └─ No suffix → execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2 in prompt)

2. CHECK if $ARGUMENTS contains an agent path:
   ├─ IF present (.opencode/agent/*.md) → target_path = detected value, omit Q0
   └─ IF missing → include Q0 in prompt

3. CHECK for --spec-folder flag:
   ├─ IF present → spec_folder = value, omit Q1
   └─ IF missing → include Q1 in prompt

4. CHECK for --iterations flag:
   ├─ IF present → max_iterations = value
   └─ IF missing → max_iterations = 5 (default)

5. List available agents for Q0:
   $ ls .opencode/agent/*.md

6. List recent spec folders for Q1:
   $ ls -d specs/*/ 2>/dev/null | tail -10

7. ASK user with SINGLE consolidated prompt (include only applicable questions):

   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ **Before proceeding, please answer:**                          │
   │                                                                │
   │ **Q0. Target Agent** (if not provided in command):             │
   │    Which agent would you like to evaluate and improve?         │
   │    [list agents found above]                                   │
   │                                                                │
   │ **Q1. Spec Folder** (if no --spec-folder flag):                 │
   │    A) Use existing spec folder: [list if found]                │
   │    B) Create new spec folder                                   │
   │    C) Use temporary directory                                  │
   │                                                                │
   │ **Q2. Execution Mode** (if no :auto/:confirm suffix):            │
   │    A) Autonomous — run loop without approval gates             │
   │    B) Interactive — confirm at each iteration                   │
   │                                                                │
   │ **Q3. Scoring Mode**:                                          │
   │    A) Dynamic — 5-dimension integration-aware (any agent)      │
   │    B) Static — legacy profile (handover/context-prime only)     │
   │                                                                │
   │ Reply format: "handover, A, A, A" or                           │
   │ ".opencode/agent/debug.md, B, B, A"                            │

    58	## 2. PROBLEM & PURPOSE
    59	
    60	### Problem Statement

Mapped the packet’s claimed phase structure, checked the runtime-facing lifecycle/graph contracts across the three skills, and compared those docs against the shipped command YAMLs, reducers, and coverage-graph handler/tests. I have enough evidence to report the traceability findings precisely.
  ✓ Read packet 042 docs to identify the claimed runtime/doc changes and referenced surfaces for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.
  ✓ Inspect the referenced skill docs, commands, agents, and runtime scripts to verify names, fields, lifecycle branches, and documented features against implementation.
  ✓ Synthesize traceability findings with severity, evidence, and precise file/line citations, plus coverage/confidence/next priorities.
codex
1. `F-019-001`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md`  
Line: `3, 35, 66-70, 80-89, 102-124, 199-204`  
Title: Parent packet still routes readers through the pre-remediation four-phase map  
Description: The root packet says the bundle now has eight child phases plus closing-audit remediation, but the same spec still describes Packet 042 as a four-phase bundle and maps Phase 7 to `007-graph-aware-stop-gate`, which does not exist. The live Phase 7 folder is `007-skill-rename-improve-agent-prompt`, and Phase 8 explicitly depends on it. The companion plan and implementation summary repeat the four-phase framing while also claiming parent-child synchronization is complete, so the packet’s own navigation layer is no longer trustworthy.  
Evidence: `spec.md:23-37` lists eight phases but names `007-graph-aware-stop-gate`; `spec.md:66-70`, `80-89`, `102-124`, `199-204` still say “four” phases. `plan.md:3`, `28`, `34`, `44`, `71-76`, `103-110` also remain four-phase. `implementation-summary.md:28`, `36`, `87`, `117` still describe a four-phase/100-file bundle and claim “Parent-child doc synchronization | PASS”. The actual Phase 7 spec is `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/007-skill-rename-improve-agent-prompt/spec.md:2-3`, and Phase 8 names it as predecessor at `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:48-49`.  
Recommendation: Reconcile the packet-root `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, and `implementation-summary.md` to the current 8-phase structure, correct the Phase 7 folder/link name, and remove all remaining “four-phase bundle” wording.

2. `F-019-002`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json`  
Line: `17-25, 49`  
Title: Deep-research runtime assets still publish removed lineage modes and the wrong archive path  
Description: The current-release lifecycle contract for `sk-deep-research` is `new | resume | restart`, with restart archiving to `research_archive/{timestamp}`. The shipped runtime template still advertises `fork` and `completed-continue` in `allowedModes` and points `archiveRoot` at `research/archive`. Companion dashboard, strategy, capability-matrix, and state-format docs repeat the removed branches. That means new configs and operator guidance can still encode lifecycle states the current workflows explicitly no longer support.  
Evidence: `deep_research_config.json:17-25,49`; `deep_research_dashboard.md:18,38`; `deep_research_strategy.md:127`; `references/capability_matrix.md:26,67-70`; `references/state_format.md:78-81`. The live contract retracts those branches in `references/loop_protocol.md:91-99`, and the actual workflows archive to `research_archive/{timestamp}` in `spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml:146-152` and `spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:152-157`.  
Recommendation: Remove `fork`/`completed-continue` from research runtime templates and references, change the archive root to `research_archive`, and align all lifecycle docs with the three-mode current-release contract.

3. `F-019-003`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml`  
Line: `147-157, 482-483`  
Title: Deep-review lifecycle contract still documents `fork` and `completed-continue` as live review modes  
Description: The review runtime now documents only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` as supported lineage modes, but the review-mode contract still defines `fork` and `completed-continue` as active lifecycle choices and even treats parity as requiring those branches. The dashboard template also still says confirm mode is shown before a `resume/restart/fork` choice. This leaves the review asset layer behind the current runtime contract and can cause operators to validate against branches that should not exist.  
Evidence: `review_mode_contract.yaml:147-157,482-483`; `deep_review_dashboard.md:18`. The current runtime contract retracts those branches in `references/loop_protocol.md:528-536`, and `references/state_format.md:114-116` says only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are emitted by the current runtime.  
Recommendation: Update the review-mode contract and dashboard template to the three supported modes, and change lifecycle-parity checks so they fail on `fork`/`completed-continue` mentions instead of requiring them.

4. `F-019-004`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_config.json`  
Line: `17-24, 78-82`  
Title: Improve-agent still ships phantom resume lineage semantics  
Description: `sk-improve-agent` now says the current release is one-session-only with a single supported lineage mode, `new`. But the runtime config still lists `resume`, `restart`, and `fork` in `allowedModes` and carries a `sessionResume` block, while the command doc still contains a “Step 6D: Resume Semantics” section telling operators to pass `--session-id=<prior-id>` and replay journal state. The same command page immediately retracts multi-generation support, so the operator docs are self-contradictory and advertise a feature that is not wired.  
Evidence: `improvement_config.json:17-24,78-82`; `SKILL.md:283-289`; `command/improve/agent.md:330-339`.  
Recommendation: Strip or clearly mark dormant resume fields from `improvement_config.json`, delete the `--session-id`/resume instructions from the command doc, and keep all improve-agent surfaces aligned with the new-only current-release contract until lineage support actually exists.

5. `F-019-005`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-truth/034-replay-consumer.md`  
Line: `13, 29-30, 84, 91, 98`  
Title: Replay-consumer docs still claim dashboard sections the reducer does not render  
Description: The playbook and release note say `/improve:agent` now exposes distinct dashboard sections for journal summary, candidate lineage, and mutation coverage. The reducer does populate `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage` in `experiment-registry.json`, but `renderDashboard()` never renders any of those sections. Manual verification therefore instructs operators to look for dashboard output that does not exist, and the changelog overstates shipped behavior.  
Evidence: `034-replay-consumer.md:13,29-30,84,91,98`; `.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md:85`. Actual reducer behavior: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:761-813,837-839` renders only Global Summary, Sample Quality, Guardrails, Stop Status, profile sections, and Recommendation, while storing the three summaries in the registry only.  
Recommendation: Either add explicit dashboard sections for those three replay-consumer summaries or rewrite the playbook/changelog/SKILL wording to say they are registry-only outputs.

6. `F-019-006`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/029-graph-events-emission.md`  
Line: `3, 14, 29-35, 50, 67-68`  
Title: Graph-events validation still points operators to an obsolete schema and stale contract test  
Description: The research and review playbooks still tell operators to validate `graphEvents` using `_node` taxonomies like `question_node`, `dimension_node`, and the old `coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts` contract stub. The live iteration contract is different: research emits flat graph events with `type: "question" | "finding" | "claim" | "source" | "edge"`, review emits `type: "dimension" | "file" | "finding" | "evidence" | "remediation" | "edge"`, and the workflow constraints tell agents to emit `{type,id,label,relation?,source?,target?}`. The cited test file is also stale: it still says the MCP implementation “will be wired in Phase 3” and models a `namespace`/`nodes.type`/`kind+nodeType` contract that no longer matches the current `handleCoverageGraphUpsert()` interface.  
Evidence: research playbook `029-graph-events-emission.md:3,14,29-35,50,67-68`; review playbook `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/03--iteration-execution-and-state-discipline/015-graph-events-review.md:3,14,29-35,50,67-68`; research state contract `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:224-238`; review state contract `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:223-259`; workflow constraints `spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml:402` and `spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:581-582`; stale test stub `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:11-16,21-25,44-50,142-175,203-213`; live handler `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:23-42`.  
Recommendation: Rewrite the graph-event playbooks and replace/retire the Phase-3-era contract stub so all validation surfaces point to the current flat `graphEvents` schema and the current upsert interface.

The live Phase 7 folder is `007-skill-rename-improve-agent-prompt`, and Phase 8 explicitly depends on it. The companion plan and implementation summary repeat the four-phase framing while also claiming parent-child synchronization is complete, so the packet’s own navigation layer is no longer trustworthy.  
Evidence: `spec.md:23-37` lists eight phases but names `007-graph-aware-stop-gate`; `spec.md:66-70`, `80-89`, `102-124`, `199-204` still say “four” phases. `plan.md:3`, `28`, `34`, `44`, `71-76`, `103-110` also remain four-phase. `implementation-summary.md:28`, `36`, `87`, `117` still describe a four-phase/100-file bundle and claim “Parent-child doc synchronization | PASS”. The actual Phase 7 spec is `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/007-skill-rename-improve-agent-prompt/spec.md:2-3`, and Phase 8 names it as predecessor at `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:48-49`.  
Recommendation: Reconcile the packet-root `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, and `implementation-summary.md` to the current 8-phase structure, correct the Phase 7 folder/link name, and remove all remaining “four-phase bundle” wording.
